_provenance: Synthetic Transaction Certificates, structurally modeled on Textile Exchange template ASR-205 and TE-TXL-POL-203 v4.0, including the real verification-URL format certificates.textileexchange.org/tc/NNNN. Data is fictional and labelled on camera.
_definition: A Transaction Certificate (TC) verifies that a SPECIFIC shipment of goods actually contains the claimed certified material. Issued at each change of ownership; details match invoices/shipping documents. This is the evidence a per-product recycled-content claim requires.

products.productName: recycled polyester filament — puffer shell fabric

transaction_certificates.note: Proves ONLY 40% certified recycled content for this specific shipment — contradicts the marketed '70% recycled polyester' claim (C1). SC is valid, but the TC does not substantiate 70%.

products.productName: organic cotton woven fabric — wrap dress

transaction_certificates.note: Substantiates the 'GOTS-certified organic cotton' claim (C3): SC valid AND TC confirms 100% organic cotton for this shipment.
Substantiates a discovered RDS down claim for GL-01: SC valid AND TC confirms 100% RDS-certified down fill for this shipment.

products.productName: RWS merino wool yarn — knit sweater

transaction_certificates.note: Substantiates a discovered RWS wool claim for GL-02: SC valid AND TC confirms 85% RWS-certified merino wool for this shipment.
